projects.description: Reproductions install astroid from PyPI via micropip into the Pyodide build that ships in the browser; pages also publish a CLI repro.py that runs against a host CPython pinned via mise.

projects.tagline: GNU bash and POSIX shell semantics.
projects.description: Layer 2 reproductions ship as Docker images: visitors run a single `docker run` command to observe the documented footgun on their own machine.
Reproductions hit Python 3.13 / sqlite3 in the same Pyodide build that ships in the browser; pages also publish a CLI repro.py that runs against a host CPython pinned via mise.
Cross-tool footguns at the find/xargs boundary, packaged as Layer 2 Docker reproductions for one-shot local replication.
Layer 2 reproductions demonstrating advisory-only lock semantics — non-flock-aware tools bypass the lock silently.
Numerical-stability bugs that survive when mpmath is installed into Pyodide via micropip. The browser page and the host-Python repro.py share the exact same script and the same pinned wheel.

projects.tagline: Node.js runtime and bundled subsystems (V8, ICU, libuv).
projects.description: Layer 2 Docker reproductions targeting open upstream regressions in the Node.js runtime. Each recipe pins a stable `node:<major>-slim` base so the verdict flips from `reproduced` to `unreproduced` once the upstream fix lands in a later patch release.
Bugs in numeric or temporal semantics that survive when numpy is loaded inside Pyodide. The browser page and the host-Python repro.py share the exact same script.
Empty / dtype-edge regressions that reproduce against the pandas wheel bundled with Pyodide; the same script also runs against host pandas via uv.
Reproductions execute under php-wasm; host parity via mise-pinned php running the same repro.php.
Layer 2 Docker reproductions targeting documented (but easily-missed) behaviours under default isolation levels.
Layer 3 record-replay reproductions: a real pthreaded program is run under rr, the recorded trace ships in the image, and replay produces a bit-identical verdict every time.
Compiled to wasm32-wasip1 with a pinned crate version, so verdicts flip when the upstream fix lands in a future regex release — turning the page into a fix-detection sentinel.

projects.tagline: Ruby standard library and language semantics.
projects.description: Reproductions run on ruby.wasm (CRuby compiled to WASI). Host parity is provided by mise-pinned ruby for the repro.rb script.
